title: Polypodium angulare
intro: Polypodium angulare, also known as Devil's Claw fern, is a type of terrestrial fern belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as arthritis, muscle and joint pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium angulare include harpagoside, a phenylpropanoid glycoside responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds like sesquiterpenes and alkaloids. In herbal medicine, Polypodium angulare is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, allowing for convenient and standardized dosing.
taxonomy: Polypodium angulare is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Polypodium, which includes over 600 species of ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with species found all over the world. Within the genus Polypodium, P. angulare is one of the species that can be found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of P. angulare reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns and its unique characteristics as a species.
uses: Polypodium angulare has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in treating wounds, cuts, and skin infections. In some traditional medicine practices, Polypodium angulare is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health conditions. The plant's extracts are also thought to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Polypodium angulare for medicinal purposes.

parts: Polypodium angulare, also known as the Angle Fern or Rough-crested Polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern. The medicinal parts of Polypodium angulare are its rhizomes, roots, and leaves, which are used in traditional medicine.
